contents.value:
```css
display: block | inline | inline-block | flex | grid | none | contents | flow-root | inline-flex | inline-grid | table | list-item;
```

Sets the display type of an element.

- `block` — takes full width, stacks vertically
- `inline` — flows with text, no width/height
- `inline-block` — inline flow with block properties
- `flex` — enables flexbox layout
- `grid` — enables grid layout
- `none` — removes from rendering
- `contents` — element box replaced by children
- `flow-root` — creates new block formatting context

```css
position: static | relative | absolute | fixed | sticky;
```

Sets how an element is positioned.

- `static` — default; normal flow
- `relative` — offset from normal position; creates positioning context
- `absolute` — removed from flow; relative to nearest positioned ancestor
- `fixed` — relative to viewport; stays in place on scroll
- `sticky` — acts as `relative` until a scroll threshold, then `fixed`

Works with `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, `inset` and `z-index`.

```css
flex-direction: row | row-reverse | column | column-reverse;
```

Defines the main axis direction for a flex container.

- `row` (default) — left to right
- `row-reverse` — right to left
- `column` — top to bottom
- `column-reverse` — bottom to top

Changing direction also swaps `justify-content` and `align-items` axes.

```css
justify-content: flex-start | flex-end | center | space-between | space-around | space-evenly;
```

Distributes space along the main axis (flex) or inline axis (grid).

- `flex-start` — items packed to start
- `center` — items centered
- `space-between` — equal space between items, no edge space
- `space-around` — equal space around items
- `space-evenly` — equal space between and around items

```css
align-items: stretch | flex-start | flex-end | center | baseline;
```

Aligns flex/grid items along the cross axis.

- `stretch` (default) — fill the container
- `center` — centered in cross axis
- `flex-start` — aligned to start of cross axis
- `baseline` — aligned by text baselines

```css
margin: <length> | <percentage> | auto;
/* 1-4 values: top right bottom left */
```

Sets the margin area around an element.

- 1 value: all sides
- 2 values: vertical | horizontal
- 3 values: top | horizontal | bottom
- 4 values: top | right | bottom | left

`margin: 0 auto;` centers a block element horizontally.
Margins can collapse vertically between adjacent block elements.

```css
padding: <length> | <percentage>;
/* 1-4 values: top right bottom left */
```

Sets the padding area between content and border.

Same shorthand pattern as `margin`. Cannot be negative.
Percentages are relative to the **width** of the containing block (even for top/bottom).

```css
border-radius: <length-percentage>{1,4} [ / <length-percentage>{1,4} ]?;
```

Rounds the corners of an element's border box.

- 1 value: all corners
- 2 values: top-left+bottom-right | top-right+bottom-left
- 4 values: top-left | top-right | bottom-right | bottom-left

Optional `/` syntax for elliptical radii.

`border-radius: 50%;` creates a circle on equal-dimension elements.

```css
z-index: auto | <integer>;
```

Sets the stacking order for positioned elements (`position` not `static`).

Higher values appear in front. Negative values are allowed.
Creates a new stacking context when applied to a positioned element.

Common convention: use increments of 10 or 100 for maintainability.

```css
@media <type>? [and <feature>]* { ... }
```

Conditional style rules based on device/viewport characteristics.

Common features:
- `width`, `min-width`, `max-width`
- `height`, `min-height`, `max-height`
- `orientation: portrait | landscape`
- `prefers-color-scheme: light | dark`
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce | no-preference`
- `hover: hover | none`
- `pointer: fine | coarse | none`

Range syntax (Level 4): `@media (400px <= width <= 800px) { }`

```css
calc(<expression>)
```

Performs math with mixed CSS units.

Operators: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`

Examples:
```css
width: calc(100% - 2rem);
font-size: calc(1rem + 0.5vw);
height: calc(100vh - var(--header-height));
margin: calc(var(--spacing) * 2);
```

Note: `+` and `-` require spaces around them. `*` and `/` do not.
Can be nested: `calc(calc(100% - 1rem) / 2)`.

```css
clamp(MIN, VAL, MAX)
```

Returns the middle value, clamped between MIN and MAX.

Equivalent to `max(MIN, min(VAL, MAX))`.

Example:
```css
font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem);
width: clamp(200px, 50%, 600px);
```

Ideal for fluid typography and responsive sizing without media queries.

```css
width: <length> | <percentage> | auto | min-content | max-content | fit-content(<length>);
```

Sets the width of an element's content box (or border box with `box-sizing: border-box`).

- `auto` — determined by content and containment
- `min-content` — shrink to narrowest content
- `max-content` — expand to widest content
- `fit-content()` — clamp between min-content and max-content

```css
height: <length> | <percentage> | auto | min-content | max-content | fit-content(<length>);
```

Sets the height of the element. Percentages resolve against the containing block's height.

`height: 100vh` fills the full viewport height.
`height: 100dvh` uses dynamic viewport height (adjusts for mobile browser chrome).

```css
min-width: <length> | <percentage> | auto | min-content | max-content | fit-content(<length>);
```

Sets the minimum width. The element will never be narrower than this value.

Overrides `width` and `max-width` when they would result in a smaller value.

```css
max-width: <length> | <percentage> | none | min-content | max-content | fit-content(<length>);
```

Sets the maximum width. The element will never be wider than this value.

Overrides `width` but is overridden by `min-width`. Common pattern: `max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto;` for centered layouts.

```css
min-height: <length> | <percentage> | auto | min-content | max-content | fit-content(<length>);
```

Sets the minimum height. Useful for ensuring content areas fill available space: `min-height: 100vh;` for full-page layouts.

```css
max-height: <length> | <percentage> | none | min-content | max-content;
```

Sets the maximum height. Commonly used with `overflow: auto` for scrollable regions.

```css
top: <length> | <percentage> | auto;
```

Specifies the vertical position of a positioned element. Behavior depends on `position`:
- `relative` — offsets from normal position
- `absolute` — distance from top edge of containing block
- `fixed` — distance from top of viewport
- `sticky` — threshold for sticking

```css
right: <length> | <percentage> | auto;
```

Specifies the horizontal position from the right edge of a positioned element's containing block.

```css
bottom: <length> | <percentage> | auto;
```

Specifies the vertical position from the bottom edge of a positioned element's containing block.

```css
left: <length> | <percentage> | auto;
```

Specifies the horizontal position from the left edge of a positioned element's containing block.

```css
flex-wrap: nowrap | wrap | wrap-reverse;
```

- `nowrap` (default) — all items on one line, may overflow
- `wrap` — items wrap to new lines from top to bottom
- `wrap-reverse` — items wrap from bottom to top

When wrapping, `align-content` controls spacing between lines.

```css
flex-grow: <number>;
```

Specifies how much a flex item grows relative to siblings when positive free space is distributed.

- `0` (default) — don't grow
- `1` — grow equally
- `2` — grow twice as much as items with `flex-grow: 1`

```css
flex-shrink: <number>;
```

Specifies how much a flex item shrinks relative to siblings when there isn't enough space.

- `1` (default) — shrink equally
- `0` — don't shrink (useful for fixed-width sidebars)

```css
flex-basis: <length> | <percentage> | auto | content;
```

Sets the initial main size of a flex item before free space is distributed.

- `auto` — uses `width`/`height` value
- `0` — ignores content size, distributes all space via `flex-grow`
- A specific length — sets an explicit starting size

```css
align-content: stretch | flex-start | flex-end | center | space-between | space-around | space-evenly;
```

Distributes space between and around flex/grid lines along the cross axis.

Only applies when there are multiple lines (flex-wrap: wrap or grid with multiple rows).

```css
align-self: auto | flex-start | flex-end | center | baseline | stretch;
```

Overrides `align-items` for a specific flex/grid item. Aligns this item independently on the cross axis.

```css
gap: <row-gap> <column-gap>?;
```

Shorthand for `row-gap` and `column-gap`. Sets gutters between flex/grid items.

- `gap: 1rem;` — uniform gap
- `gap: 1rem 2rem;` — row gap | column gap

Replaces the older `grid-gap` property. Works in both Flexbox and Grid.

```css
grid-column: <start-line> / <end-line>;
```

Shorthand for `grid-column-start` / `grid-column-end`. Places a grid item horizontally.

Examples:
- `grid-column: 1 / 3;` — spans columns 1 and 2
- `grid-column: 1 / -1;` — spans all columns
- `grid-column: span 2;` — spans 2 columns from auto-placement

```css
grid-row: <start-line> / <end-line>;
```

Shorthand for `grid-row-start` / `grid-row-end`. Places a grid item vertically.

Examples:
- `grid-row: 1 / 3;` — spans rows 1 and 2
- `grid-row: span 2;` — spans 2 rows

```css
grid-auto-flow: row | column | dense | row dense | column dense;
```

Controls how auto-placed items flow into the grid.

- `row` (default) — fill rows first
- `column` — fill columns first
- `dense` — fill gaps in earlier cells (may reorder visually)

```css
place-items: <align-items> <justify-items>?;
```

Shorthand for `align-items` and `justify-items`. Centers grid/flex items:

`place-items: center;` centers items both horizontally and vertically.

```css
place-content: <align-content> <justify-content>?;
```

Shorthand for `align-content` and `justify-content`. Distributes space in both axes at once.

```css
font-size: <absolute> | <relative> | <length> | <percentage>;
```

Absolute keywords: `xx-small`, `x-small`, `small`, `medium`, `large`, `x-large`, `xx-large`.
Relative: `smaller`, `larger`.

Best practice for responsive text: `font-size: clamp(1rem, 2.5vw, 2rem);`

`em` is relative to parent font-size. `rem` is relative to root (`<html>`) font-size.

```css
font-weight: normal | bold | bolder | lighter | 100-900;
```

Numeric scale (100–900 in increments of 100):
- 100: Thin
- 300: Light
- 400: Normal (= `normal`)
- 500: Medium
- 700: Bold (= `bold`)
- 900: Black

Variable fonts support any integer in the 1–1000 range.

```css
font-family: <family-name> | <generic-family>;
```

Generic families: `serif`, `sans-serif`, `monospace`, `cursive`, `fantasy`, `system-ui`, `ui-serif`, `ui-sans-serif`, `ui-monospace`.

Always end with a generic fallback:
```css
font-family: 'Inter', 'Segoe UI', system-ui, sans-serif;
```

Quote family names containing spaces or special characters.

```css
line-height: normal | <number> | <length> | <percentage>;
```

Sets the height of a line box. Unitless values (like `1.5`) are recommended as they scale with font-size.

- `normal` — browser default (~1.2)
- `1.5` — 1.5× the font-size
- `24px` — fixed line height

Affects vertical rhythm and readability. WCAG recommends at least 1.5 for body text.

```css
letter-spacing: normal | <length>;
```

Sets the space between characters. Negative values tighten text.

Common usage:
- Headings: `letter-spacing: -0.025em;` (tighter)
- Uppercase text: `letter-spacing: 0.05em;` (wider)
- Body text: `letter-spacing: normal;`

```css
text-align: left | right | center | justify | start | end;
```

Aligns inline content within a block element.

- `start`/`end` — logical values that respect writing direction (RTL-safe)
- `justify` — stretches lines to fill width; last line is left-aligned

```css
text-decoration: <line> <style>? <color>? <thickness>?;
```

Shorthand for `text-decoration-line`, `text-decoration-style`, `text-decoration-color`, `text-decoration-thickness`.

Lines: `none`, `underline`, `overline`, `line-through`.
Styles: `solid`, `double`, `dotted`, `dashed`, `wavy`.

`text-underline-offset` adjusts position of underlines.

```css
text-transform: none | capitalize | uppercase | lowercase | full-width;
```

- `capitalize` — first letter of each word uppercased
- `uppercase` — all characters uppercased
- `lowercase` — all characters lowercased

Note: Doesn't change the actual content, only the visual presentation.

```css
white-space: normal | nowrap | pre | pre-wrap | pre-line | break-spaces;
```

- `normal` — collapse whitespace, wrap at available width
- `nowrap` — collapse whitespace, no wrapping
- `pre` — preserve whitespace and newlines, no wrapping
- `pre-wrap` — preserve whitespace, wrap at available width
- `pre-line` — collapse whitespace, preserve newlines, wrap

```css
word-break: normal | break-all | keep-all | break-word;
```

- `normal` — break at allowed break points
- `break-all` — break anywhere (for CJK text or long strings)
- `keep-all` — don't break CJK characters
- `break-word` — break at word boundaries, then anywhere if needed (deprecated, use `overflow-wrap: anywhere` instead)

```css
overflow-wrap: normal | break-word | anywhere;
```

Allows unbreakable strings to be broken to prevent overflow.

- `break-word` — break long words at arbitrary points if needed
- `anywhere` — like `break-word` but also considers soft wrap opportunities for min-content sizing

```css
color: <color>;
```

Sets the foreground color for text content and text decorations.

Color formats:
- Named: `red`, `rebeccapurple`
- Hex: `#ff0000`, `#f00`, `#ff000080`
- RGB: `rgb(255 0 0)`, `rgb(255 0 0 / 50%)`
- HSL: `hsl(0 100% 50%)`, `hsl(0 100% 50% / 50%)`
- OKLCH: `oklch(70% 0.25 30)`
- Keywords: `currentColor`, `transparent`, `inherit`

```css
background-color: <color>;
```

Sets the background color. Painted behind `background-image`.

Supports all color formats including alpha transparency:
`background-color: rgb(0 0 0 / 50%);`

```css
background-size: auto | <length> | <percentage> | cover | contain;
```

- `cover` — scales image to fill element (may crop)
- `contain` — scales image to fit entirely (may leave gaps)
- `auto` — natural image dimensions

Two values: `background-size: width height;`

```css
background-position: <position>;
```

Sets the initial position of a background image.

Keywords: `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`, `center`.
Values: `background-position: 50% 50%;` (centered)
Multi-value: `background-position: right 10px bottom 20px;`

```css
linear-gradient(<angle> | to <side>, <color-stop>, ...)
```

Creates a linear color transition.

Angles: `0deg` (up), `90deg` (right), `180deg` (down), `270deg` (left).
Keywords: `to right`, `to bottom right`, etc.

Examples:
```css
linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%);
linear-gradient(to right, red, transparent);
linear-gradient(to bottom, #000 0%, #000 50%, #fff 50%, #fff 100%);
```

```css
radial-gradient(<shape> <size> at <position>, <color-stop>, ...)
```

Creates a radial color transition from a center point.

Shapes: `circle`, `ellipse` (default).
Sizes: `closest-side`, `farthest-side`, `closest-corner`, `farthest-corner`.

Example: `radial-gradient(circle at top left, #667eea, transparent);`

```css
border-width: <length>{1,4};
```

Sets the width of all four borders. Uses the same 1-to-4 value shorthand as `margin`.

Keywords: `thin` (~1px), `medium` (~3px), `thick` (~5px).

Individual sides: `border-top-width`, `border-right-width`, etc.

```css
border-style: none | solid | dashed | dotted | double | groove | ridge | inset | outset | hidden;
```

Sets the line style of all four borders. `none` and `hidden` both render no border, but `hidden` wins in border-collapsing table cells.

```css
border-color: <color>{1,4};
```

Sets the color of all four borders. Uses 1-to-4 value shorthand.

Defaults to `currentColor` if not specified.

```css
outline: <width> <style> <color>;
```

Draws a line outside the element's border. Does NOT affect layout (no space taken).

Unlike `border`, `outline` doesn't have corners — use `outline-offset` to push it away from the element.

`outline: 2px solid blue;` is the default focus indicator in many browsers.
`outline: none;` removes it (only do this with a visible alternative).

```css
mix-blend-mode: normal | multiply | screen | overlay | darken | lighten | color-dodge | color-burn | hard-light | soft-light | difference | exclusion | hue | saturation | color | luminosity;
```

Defines how an element's content blends with the content behind it.

`isolation: isolate;` on a parent creates a new stacking context, preventing blend from affecting ancestors.
```css object-fit: fill | contain | cover | none | scale-down; ``` Sets how ``, `

```css
object-position: <position>;
```

Sets the alignment of replaced content within its box. Uses the same syntax as `background-position`.

Default: `50% 50%` (centered).
Example: `object-position: top center;`

```css
aspect-ratio: auto | <width> / <height>;
```

Sets a preferred aspect ratio for the element's box.

Examples:
- `aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;` — widescreen video
- `aspect-ratio: 1;` — square
- `aspect-ratio: 4 / 3;` — classic photo

Works with `width: 100%;` to create responsive containers that maintain their ratio.

```css
overflow-x: visible | hidden | clip | scroll | auto;
```

Controls horizontal overflow behavior.
`overflow-x: auto;` adds a scrollbar only when content overflows horizontally.

```css
overflow-y: visible | hidden | clip | scroll | auto;
```

Controls vertical overflow behavior.
`overflow-y: auto;` adds a scrollbar only when content overflows vertically.

```css
visibility: visible | hidden | collapse;
```

- `visible` — element is visible
- `hidden` — element is invisible but still occupies space (unlike `display: none`)
- `collapse` — for table elements, removes row/column; behaves like `hidden` for others

Hidden elements still receive events and can be tab-focused.

```css
pointer-events: auto | none;
```

- `auto` — element responds to pointer events normally
- `none` — element is "transparent" to pointer events; clicks pass through to elements below

Useful for overlays, disabled states, and decorative elements.

```css
user-select: auto | text | none | all | contain;
```

Controls whether text can be selected.

- `none` — prevents text selection (useful for UI elements)
- `all` — entire element selected on click (useful for code blocks)
- `text` — text can be selected normally

```css
resize: none | both | horizontal | vertical | block | inline;
```

Sets whether an element is resizable by the user.

Requires `overflow` other than `visible`. `<textarea>` is resizable by default.

```css
scroll-behavior: auto | smooth;
```

Sets whether scrolling is instant or animated.

`html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }` enables smooth scrolling for all anchor links and `scrollTo()` calls.

```css
will-change: auto | <property>+;
```

Hints to the browser about upcoming changes for optimization.

Examples:
- `will-change: transform;` — optimizes for transform animations
- `will-change: opacity;` — optimizes for opacity changes

Use sparingly — add before animation, remove after. Excessive use wastes GPU memory.

```css
contain: none | strict | content | size | layout | style | paint;
```

Indicates containment restrictions to the browser for performance optimization.

- `layout` — element's layout is independent of the rest of the page
- `paint` — descendants are not rendered outside the element's bounds
- `size` — element's size is not affected by its descendants
- `content` — shorthand for `layout paint style`
- `strict` — shorthand for `layout paint size style`

```css
accent-color: auto | <color>;
```

Sets the accent color for form controls: checkboxes, radio buttons, range sliders, and progress bars.

`accent-color: auto;` uses the browser/OS default.

Simplifies form theming without custom styling.

```css
color-scheme: normal | light | dark | light dark;
```

Indicates which color schemes the element supports.

`:root { color-scheme: light dark; }` tells the browser both are supported, enabling native dark mode for form controls, scrollbars, and other UA-rendered elements.

```css
min(<value>, <value>, ...)
```

Returns the smallest value from a comma-separated list.

Example: `width: min(100%, 600px);` — never exceeds 600px but also never exceeds parent width.

Equivalent to `max-width`-like behavior without a separate property.

```css
max(<value>, <value>, ...)
```

Returns the largest value from a comma-separated list.

Example: `width: max(300px, 50%);` — at least 300px but grows with parent.

Equivalent to `min-width`-like behavior without a separate property.

```css
:has(<selector>)
```

The "parent selector" — matches an element if any of its descendants match the argument selector.

Examples:
```css
.card:has(img) { padding: 0; }
.form:has(:invalid) { border-color: red; }
h2:has(+ p) { margin-bottom: 0; } /* h2 followed by p */
```

Highly powerful but use carefully for performance — browser evaluates from the subject outward.

```css
:is(<selector-list>)
```

Matches any element that can be matched by one of the selectors in the list. Simplifies complex selectors.

```css
:is(h1, h2, h3) { color: blue; }
:is(.card, .panel) :is(h2, h3) { margin: 0; }
```

Specificity: takes the **most specific** selector in the list. Use `:where()` for zero-specificity version.

```css
:where(<selector-list>)
```

Same as `:is()` but with **zero specificity**. Ideal for default stylesheets that should be easily overridable.

```css
:where(.dialog, .modal) { margin: auto; }
```
